title: selaginella lechleri
intro: Selaginella lechleri, also known as 'Mangosteen of the Andes', is a type of flowering plant native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountains, belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections. The health benefits of Selaginella lechleri include its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds, burns, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress levels. In herbal preparations, Selaginella lechleri is often combined with other plants to create topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as teas and tinctures that can be taken internally to promote overall health and well-being.
